prerequisites: An AppsFlyer account with admin access, since the API V2 token can only be generated and viewed by an account admin in the dashboard.
The app already added to the AppsFlyer dashboard, so you have its App ID (app_id) to replicate data for.

The App ID of the app whose data you want to pull: an Android App ID is the package name (for example com.coolcompany.coolapp) and an iOS App ID is the App Store ID prefixed with id (for example id123456789).
A start_date marking the earliest date from which to replicate data.
Optionally, the reporting timezone you want the data returned in.

authMethods.name: API V2 token (Bearer token)
authMethods.summary: Maps to the connector's api_token secret field. AppsFlyer authenticates API requests with an API V2 token, a JWT-based bearer token sent in the Authorization: Bearer <token> HTTP header. The V2 token replaced the deprecated V1 token, which was passed inside the request URL. An account admin generates the token from the AppsFlyer dashboard.

setupSteps.title: Generate the API V2 token in the AppsFlyer dashboard
setupSteps.body: Sign in to the AppsFlyer dashboard with an account admin user. Open the account menu by clicking your email address in the top bar, then select Security Center. Under AppsFlyer API tokens, click Manage your AppsFlyer API tokens and copy the API Token V2.0. Treat the token as a secret: AppsFlyer recommends replacing tokens roughly every 180 days and never publishing them in public code repositories.

setupSteps.title: Provide the API token
setupSteps.body: Paste the copied API V2 token into the connector's api_token field. The connector sends it as an Authorization: Bearer <token> header on each AppsFlyer API request.

setupSteps.title: Set the App ID
setupSteps.body: Enter the unique App ID of the app you want to replicate into the app_id field. This is the app identifier shown in the AppsFlyer dashboard: for Android it is the package name (for example com.coolcompany.coolapp) and for iOS it is the App Store ID prefixed with id (for example id123456789).

setupSteps.title: Set the start date and timezone
setupSteps.body: Set start_date to the earliest date from which the connector should replicate data. Optionally set timezone to the reporting timezone in which you want AppsFlyer to return the data; leave it blank to use the default.

sources.title: Pull API Overview (Authentication: API V2 bearer token required; admin gets the V2 token in the dashboard) - AppsFlyer developer hub
Bulletin: API token changes (V1 token deprecated in favor of JWT-based V2 token) - AppsFlyer Help Center
Managing AppsFlyer tokens (Security Center > AppsFlyer API tokens; replace every 180 days; do not publish) - AppsFlyer Help Center
AppsFlyer Help Center glossary (Android App ID = package name; iOS App ID = id + App Store ID)
AppsFlyer source - Airbyte (api_token, app_id, start_date, timezone config fields)
